title: Doubly Sub-linear Interactive Proofs of Proximity
gist: An ITCS paper (Noga Amir, Oded Goldreich, Guy N. Rothblum; Weizmann Institute, via Apple ML Research) introduces doubly sub-linear interactive proofs of proximity (dsIPPs), where both proof generation and verification read only a small fraction of a huge input, and constructs such proofs for properties decidable by constant-width read-once oblivious branching programs, Hamming weight verification, and a relaxed bipartiteness property.

insights.claim: Doubly sub-linear interactive proofs of proximity (dsIPPs) are proof systems where the honest prover only needs to read a small (sub-linear) portion of a huge input to generate a proof, and the verifier reads an even smaller portion to approximately check it -- approximate meaning the honest prover can always make the verifier accept a true instance, but no prover can fool the verifier into accepting an input that is far from having the property.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
entities: doubly sub-linear interactive proofs of proximity
insights.so_what: Establishes the core primitive (both prover and verifier sub-linear in input size) needed to make verification of claims about massive datasets computationally tractable -- foundational for any future system that wants to cheaply audit claims about huge inputs (e.g., large training datasets or model outputs) without reading them in full.
insights.quote: We study doubly sub-linear interactive proofs of proximity (dsIPPs): proofs that are ultra-fast to generate, and can be used to prove approximate assertions about a huge input.

insights.claim: Such proof systems let a party prove and verify claims about a huge input object even though the prover itself cannot read the entire object, and the verifier is even more resource-constrained than the prover.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
entities: doubly sub-linear interactive proofs of proximity
insights.so_what: A striking property -- neither party needs full read access to the data -- which could underpin future privacy-preserving or bandwidth-constrained verification schemes for extremely large datasets.
insights.quote: Such proof systems can be used to prove (and verify) claims about the properties of a huge input object, even though the prover cannot read the entire object (and the verifier is even more restricted).

insights.claim: The authors construct dsIPPs for any property decidable by a constant-width read-once oblivious branching program (ROOBP), a broad and well-studied model of bounded-memory computation.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
entities: doubly sub-linear interactive proofs of proximity
insights.so_what: Extending dsIPPs to the ROOBP computation class gives a general-purpose recipe applicable to any property expressible in that model, rather than a one-off bespoke proof system.
insights.quote: We construct such a proof system for any property that can be decided by a constant-width read-once oblivious branching program (ROOBP).

insights.claim: The paper also constructs dsIPPs for approximate verification of an input's Hamming weight, and for a relaxation of the bipartiteness property in the bounded-degree graph model.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
entities: doubly sub-linear interactive proofs of proximity
insights.so_what: Two additional concrete, named applications (Hamming weight, bipartiteness) demonstrate the framework's versatility across combinatorial and graph-theoretic properties, useful reference points for researchers extending the technique to new domains.
insights.quote: We also construct proof systems for approximate verification of an input’s Hamming weight, and for a relaxation of bipartiteness in the bounded-degree graph model.

themes.theme: Foundational role in Apple's verifiable-computation research line

insights.claim: This paper establishes the dsIPP framework that later Apple-affiliated FOCS 2026 papers build on -- one extending it to general distribution properties decidable by bounded-depth circuits, another showing the testing/verification relationship diverges between location-invariant function properties and distribution properties.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Indicates a sustained, multi-paper Apple-affiliated research program in verifiable-computation theory (not a one-off publication), suggesting long-horizon institutional investment in this theoretical area.

top_entities: doubly sub-linear interactive proofs of proximity
